title: rosa canina subsp. dumetorum
intro: Rosa canina subsp. dumetorum, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, a type of wild rose native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and wounds. The health benefits of Rosa canina subsp. dumetorum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, flowers, and hips are often used to make teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
